Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, primarily driven by the relentless expansion of the AI capex cycle across semiconductors, software, and infrastructure. While some sub-industries show mixed signals, the dominant narrative is strong demand for AI-related hardware and software solutions.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Global chip sales growth decelerates below 15% YoY
US-China tech tensions escalate with new, broader export controls

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is mixed, with some segments facing headwinds from persistent inflation and cautious consumer spending, while others show resilience or benefit from specific trends. High fuel prices and broader inflation are weighing on discretionary spending.
watchFor: Core CPI print below 0.2% MoM — signals easing inflation and potential rate cuts
Retail sales growth accelerates above 0.5% MoM
Consumer confidence index drops below 90

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed, with strong performance in digital content and AI-driven platforms, but traditional entertainment and advertising segments face evolving consumer habits and competitive pressures.
watchFor: Major ad spending budgets are cut by >10% QoQ
Streaming subscriber growth for top platforms turns negative
New regulatory actions impact content distribution or data usage

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is mixed, with strong tailwinds from defense spending and AI-driven demand for certain machinery, but facing headwinds in traditional manufacturing and logistics due to economic uncertainty.
watchFor: Global geopolitical tensions de-escalate significantly
Manufacturing PMI drops below 48 for two consecutive months
Major infrastructure spending bills are delayed or cut

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is bullish, predominantly driven by the transformative GLP-1 obesity drug wave and stable demand for healthcare plans, despite some sub-industries facing specific challenges.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy or safety profiles
Increased regulatory pressure on drug pricing or insurance reimbursement
Healthcare utilization rates decline unexpectedly

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed, with strong performance in asset management and credit services, but diversified banks face evolving regulatory landscapes and competition from fintech, alongside broader macro interest rate uncertainty.
watchFor: Fed signals clear dovish pivot, leading to steepening yield curve
Credit quality deteriorates significantly across consumer or corporate loans
New financial regulations increase compliance costs for banks

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is holding steady, with discount stores benefiting from inflation-conscious consumers, but facing broader pressures from rising prices impacting purchasing power.
watchFor: Core CPI print below 0.2% MoM — reduces demand for discount options
Real wage growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters
Consumer staple companies report significant margin compression

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is bullish, primarily driven by the surging power demand from AI data centers, creating new opportunities for independent power producers and renewable energy providers.
watchFor: AI data center buildout slows or shifts geographically
Regulatory approvals for new power generation or transmission are delayed
Interest rates rise significantly, increasing cost of capital for infrastructure projects

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector is neutral, with specific industrial metals facing mixed signals from geopolitical factors and demand shifts, while broader commodity prices are influenced by global economic outlook.
watchFor: Global manufacturing PMI drops below 48
US Dollar index strengthens significantly
Major supply chain disruptions impact commodity availability

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are currently neutral, with gold and silver prices influenced by a complex interplay of inflation expectations, real yields, dollar strength, and geopolitical risk, without a clear dominant trend.
watchFor: Real yields turn significantly negative
US Dollar index weakens below 100
Major geopolitical conflict escalates unexpectedly
